scope_note: Two distinct concepts organized in one file: (1) eight world-wide LAYER TYPE CATEGORIES, a standard World Partition organizational axis for toggling whole content categories; (2) the compiled DISTRICT GATING LAYER inventory, the union of all story/ability-gated layers already declared in the five approved packages. These are complementary axes, not duplicates of each other.

layer_type_categories.description: All road-hierarchy geometry: radial arterials, ring roads, service/maintenance routes.
All district and sub-location structural geometry across all five approved districts.
Live Network and Dead Network lines, stations, and interchanges.
Mission-critical content, boss encounters, and state-gated sub-locations across all districts.
The five-district lighting spectrum, including Sector 0's uniquely dynamic story-tied lighting states.
Materials, color language, and environmental storytelling assets per district.
layer_type_categories.cross_reference: Each district's own Materials / Color Language / Environmental Storytelling sections.

layer_type_categories.description: Power, water/climate control, and maintenance systems per district.
Pedestrian, vehicle, metro, underground, and rooftop traversal rules per district.

district_gating_layers.notes: DL_Sector0_ArchivistReadingRoom_PostGame is precisely the content-state gating WorldPartition.json's cell_relay_stations flagged as belonging here rather than to a separate cell — confirmed consistent.

cross_district_layers.description: Fragments are collected across every district but visualized specifically via Sector 0's relay-lighting system (DL_Sector0_RelayFragmentLighting_Progressive). This layer is the shared counter both systems depend on.

layer_interaction_rules.type_categories_and_gating_layers_are_in: A district-gating layer (e.g. DL_WhiteZone_RooftopMechanical_EchoShiftGated) belongs to multiple type categories simultaneously (building_layer, gameplay_layer, navigation_layer) — the two axes are not mutually exclusive and the Builder should not treat them as a single hierarchy.
layer_interaction_rules.gating_precedence: Story/ability gate conditions (district_gating_layers) always take precedence over type-category visibility toggles (layer_type_categories) — a type category being 'on' never bypasses a narrative gate.
layer_interaction_rules.cross_district_layer_precedence: DL_Global_MemoryFragmentProgress reads from, but never writes to, any individual district's own gating layers — it is a downstream aggregator only.
